content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第6页第三部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 3 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 第三部分。按照导师的要求，你将听到
content.en_text: Section 3 Following instructions from their tutor, you

content.cn_text: 两名经济学学生 James 和 Anna 讨论一项近期关于
content.en_text: will hear two economic students called James and Anna discussing a recent survey about

content.cn_text: 新西兰购房成本的调查。
content.en_text: the cost of buying a house in New Zealand.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第6页的第21至第25题。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第25题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: Tutor：好的，这周的作业你们要评估关于房价的 Value Survey。
content.en_text: Tutor: Okay, for this week's assignment you're going to assess the Value Survey on house prices.

content.cn_text: 这项调查有哪些发现？
content.en_text: What were the survey's findings?

content.cn_text: 这项调查可靠吗？
content.en_text: Was it a reliable survey?

content.cn_text: 两人一组，讨论你们读过的内容，并为作业提出一些想法。
content.en_text: Working pairs, discuss what you've read and generate some ideas for the assignment.

content.cn_text: James：好的，Anna，我们一起做吧？
content.en_text: James: All right, shall we work together Anna?

content.cn_text: Anna：当然，James。首先，Value Survey 实际上是如何衡量房屋成本的？
content.en_text: Anna: Sure James, so first, how did the Value Survey actually measure the cost of houses?

content.cn_text: 因为计算方式有很多种。
content.en_text: Because there are lots of ways of calculating it.

content.cn_text: James：嗯，它看的是所有房屋，对吧？
content.en_text: James: Well, it looked at all houses, didn't it?

content.cn_text: 新房和旧房。
content.en_text: New and old.

content.cn_text: 所以它先从整个劳动力的平均收入入手，然后计算房价
content.en_text: So it started with average incomes across the whole workforce and then worked out house prices

content.cn_text: 与这一收入的关系，也就是与人们收入的关系。
content.en_text: in relation to that, I mean to what people were earning.

content.cn_text: Anna：所以它关注的是可负担性，也就是人们实际支付的成本，并没有关于
content.en_text: Anna: So it's about affordability, what people pay in real terms, there's no information about

content.cn_text: 一个普通家庭能买多大房子的信息。
content.en_text: the size of a house, a typical family can buy.

content.cn_text: James：没有，这是一个局限。
content.en_text: James: No, that's a limitation.

content.cn_text: 好的，那么在作业中我们应该提供一些背景，也就是 Value Survey 有什么不同，
content.en_text: Okay, then in the assignment we should give some background, I mean what's different

content.cn_text: 它特别在哪里？
content.en_text: about the Value Survey?

content.cn_text: Anna：是的，每年都有很多这类调查。
content.en_text: Anna: Yeah, there's lots of these surveys every year.

content.cn_text: Value Survey 实际上是较新的调查之一，但它很突出，因为它提供了
content.en_text: The Value is actually one of the newer ones, but it stands out because it gives figures

content.cn_text: 一系列最大城市中心的数据，而其他调查都没有做到这一点。
content.en_text: for a range of the largest urban centres, which none of the others do.

content.cn_text: James：好的，这点不错。
content.en_text: James: Okay, good point.

content.cn_text: 然后其中一个发现是，新西兰房屋相对昂贵。
content.en_text: Then one finding was that New Zealand houses are relatively expensive.

content.cn_text: 为什么会这样？
content.en_text: Why is that?

content.cn_text: 因为大多数房屋是木头建的，而木材相当便宜。
content.en_text: Because most of them are built of wood, and that's quite cheap.

content.cn_text: Anna：是的，确实。
content.en_text: Anna: Yeah, it is.

content.cn_text: 但问题是，关于哪里可以建房有太多规定和限制。
content.en_text: But the problem is, there are too many regulations and restrictions about where you can build.

content.cn_text: James: 所以如果为住房创造更多建筑用地，成本就会下降。
content.en_text: James: So if more building sites were created for housing, the costs would come down.

content.cn_text: 就是这样，对吧？
content.en_text: That's it, isn't it?

content.cn_text: 因为有很多承包商已经准备好做这些工作。
content.en_text: Because there are plenty of contractors ready to do the work.

content.cn_text: Anna：没错，还有什么？
content.en_text: Anna: Exactly, so what else?

content.cn_text: James：嗯，交通方面呢？
content.en_text: James: Well, what about transport?

content.cn_text: 我是说，谈论新住房当然很好，但人们要怎么到那里去呢？
content.en_text: I mean, it's all very well to talk about new housing, but how are people going to get

content.cn_text: 到那里？
content.en_text: there?

content.cn_text: 调查有没有提出新道路、新铁路网络？
content.en_text: Is the survey proposing new roads, new rail networks?

content.cn_text: 它应该是一个协调一致的方案。
content.en_text: It should be a co-ordinated approach.

content.cn_text: Anna：是的，但调查没有提到这一点。
content.en_text: Anna: Yeah, but the survey didn't mention it.

content.cn_text: 这是一个错失的机会，因为如果从一开始就承诺为公共交通提供资金，
content.en_text: It's a missed opportunity because a commitment to funding public transport from the outset

content.cn_text: 就会改善我们城市的空气质量。
content.en_text: would improve air quality in our cities.

content.cn_text: James：没错。
content.en_text: James: True.

content.cn_text: 好的，那么。
content.en_text: Okay, then.

content.cn_text: 下一次调查，我们可以期待什么？
content.en_text: The next survey, what can we expect?

content.cn_text: 它会考察所有相同的地点，对吧？
content.en_text: It'll look at all the same places, right?

content.cn_text: Anna：完全相同。
content.en_text: Anna: Exactly the same.

content.cn_text: 但下一次调查会让人了解价格走势。
content.en_text: But the next one will give an idea of the way prices are moving.

content.cn_text: James: 政府可能必须思考房价如何影响社会。
content.en_text: James: The government may have to think about how house prices are affecting society.

content.cn_text: 在听讨论的剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第26至第30题，
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 26-30

content.cn_text: 在第7页。
content.en_text: on page 7.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Now, listen and answer questions 26-30.

content.cn_text: Anna：接下来在作业中，我们需要评估这项调查的可靠性。
content.en_text: Anna: Then in the assignment, we need to evaluate how reliable the survey was.

content.cn_text: 所以我们应该看看评论者提出的一些批评。
content.en_text: So we should look at some of the criticism made by commentators.

content.cn_text: 好的。
content.en_text: Okay.

content.cn_text: 有经济学家 Andrew Coleman。
content.en_text: So there's the economist Andrew Coleman.

content.cn_text: 他实际上质疑这项调查的钱是否花得值得，因为它只
content.en_text: He actually questioned whether the survey was money well spent, because it only looked

content.cn_text: 孤立地考察了单独一年。
content.en_text: at a single year in isolation.

content.cn_text: James：而那一年借钱买房很困难，从而推高了成本。
content.en_text: James：A year when it was difficult to borrow money to buy a house, pushing up costs.

content.cn_text: 所以他认为调查应该考虑更长的时间跨度。
content.en_text: So he thought the survey should consider a longer time frame.

content.cn_text: 你拿到 Massey 教授那篇文章了吗？
content.en_text: Did you get that article by Professor Massey?

content.cn_text: 因为他是这个主题的权威。
content.en_text: Because he's the leading authority on this topic.

content.cn_text: Anna：拿到了，很有意思。
content.en_text: Anna: Yeah, interesting.

content.cn_text: 他意识到用于计算人们有多少钱的方法没有把储蓄
content.en_text: He realised the method used to calculate how much money people had, didn't take savings

content.cn_text: 考虑进去。
content.en_text: into account.

content.cn_text: 所以实际上，寻找房子的人比调查显示的更有钱，房屋
content.en_text: So actually, people looking for a house were richer than the survey shows, making houses

content.cn_text: 实际意义上就更便宜。
content.en_text: cheaper in real terms.

content.cn_text: James：好的。
content.en_text: James：All right.

content.cn_text: 然后是 Richard Bernard，他的批评很有新意。
content.en_text: Then Richard Bernard, his criticism was original.

content.cn_text: 他发现他们使用相当过时的软件来处理所有收集到的信息，
content.en_text: He found out they were using quite outdated software to process all the information they

content.cn_text: 这很离谱，因为他们其实有资金进行升级。
content.en_text: gathered, which was crazy because they actually had the money available for an upgrade.

content.cn_text: 所以即使研究团队很受尊重，统计数据的
content.en_text: So even though it was a highly regarded team of people, the way the statistics were

content.cn_text: 解读方式仍然值得质疑。
content.en_text: interpreted was open to question.

content.cn_text: Anna：好的。
content.en_text: Anna: Okay.

content.cn_text: 然后是 Lowndes 教授，他认为报告写得很糟。
content.en_text: Then Professor Lowndes, he thought the report was just badly written.

content.cn_text: James：没错。
content.en_text: James: That's right.

content.cn_text: 在花了大量工作收集有效信息之后，最终报告
content.en_text: After all the work that went into gathering valid information, the finished report was

content.cn_text: 太长且结构薄弱，还有太多不必要的细节。
content.en_text: just too long and weakly structured, and it was too much unnecessary detail.

content.cn_text: Anna：最后一位是 Maria Darling，她对被评估的房产提出了看法。
content.en_text: Anna: And the last one, Maria Darling, she made a point about the properties that were evaluated.

content.cn_text: James：你是说数量不够吗？
content.en_text: James： They weren't enough of them, you mean?

content.cn_text: Anna：不是，不是这个问题，而是出于隐私原因，这些房产从未被说明身份。
content.en_text: Anna: No, it wasn't that, but they were never identified for privacy reasons.

content.cn_text: 但她认为实际使用的房产应该被公开，你懂吗？
content.en_text: But she thought the actual properties used should have been revealed, you know?

content.cn_text: 如果人们要对调查有充分信心，这个过程就应该透明。
content.en_text: The process should have been transparent if the people are going to have full confidence

content.cn_text: 对调查有充分信心。
content.en_text: in the survey.

content.cn_text: James：对。
content.en_text: James： Right.

content.cn_text: 所以批评很多。
content.en_text: So a lot of criticism.

content.cn_text: Anna：是的。
content.en_text: Anna: Yeah.

content.cn_text: 但这类研究通常都有争议。
content.en_text: But this sort of research is often controversial.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of Section 3.

content.cn_text: 现在你有半分钟检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21–25

items.title: According to James and Anna, the Value Survey measured the cost of houses by calculating

options.title: A
options.content: the total price of building a new house.

options.title: B
options.content: the size of house a typical family can afford.

options.title: C
options.content: the price of houses compared to average income.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Value Survey 如何衡量房屋成本。原文说从 average incomes 开始，再计算 house prices in relation to what people were earning，所以答案是 C. the price of houses compared to average income。

items.title: Anna says the Value Survey is unusual because

options.title: A
options.content: it compares prices in different cities.

options.title: B
options.content: it is the oldest survey of its kind.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Anna 说 Value Survey 特别在哪里。原文说它给出 a range of the largest urban centres 的数据，其他调查没有，也就是比较不同城市的价格，所以答案是 A. it compares prices in different cities。

items.title: The students decide that New Zealand house prices are high because of
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问学生们认为新西兰房价高的原因。原文说有 too many regulations and restrictions about where you can build，所以答案是 C. the rules concerning land use。

items.title: When the students talk about transport they are
items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问学生们谈到交通时的态度。Anna 说 should be a co-ordinated approach，James 说 survey didn't mention it，说明他们担心没有计划，所以答案是 A. concerned there is no plan。

items.title: The students agree that the next Value Survey will

options.title: A
options.content: compare a wider number of locations.

options.title: B
options.content: indicate a future trend in house prices.

options.title: C
options.content: advise against regulation of house prices.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问学生们同意下一次 Value Survey 会怎样。原文说 next one will give an idea of the way prices are moving，所以答案是 B. indicate a future trend in house prices。

groups.desc: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–G, next to questions 26–30.
groups.title: Questions 26–30

list.title: E
list.content: lack of public access to full information

items.title: Andrew Coleman
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 Andrew Coleman 批评了调查的哪个方面。原文说 only looked at a single year，应该 consider a longer time frame，所以答案是 C. period covered。

items.title: Professor Massey
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问 Professor Massey 批评了调查的哪个方面。原文说计算人们有多少钱的方法 didn't take savings into account，说明对购房者财富的定义有问题，所以答案是 G. way of defining home-buyers' wealth。

items.title: Richard Bernard
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问 Richard Bernard 批评了调查的哪个方面。原文说 they were using quite outdated software to process information，所以答案是 F. old-fashioned systems of data analysis。

items.title: Professor Lowndes
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问 Professor Lowndes 批评了调查的哪个方面。原文说 report was badly written, too long and weakly structured，所以答案是 A. organization of final text。

items.title: Maria Darling
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问 Maria Darling 批评了调查的哪个方面。原文说 actual properties should have been revealed，process should have been transparent，所以答案是 E. lack of public access to full information。
